title: Rudra Mahalaya Temple
tagline: Rudra Mahalaya Temple — spiritual town of Gujarat

overview.short: The Rudra Mahalaya Temple, also known as Rudramal, is a destroyed/desecrated Hindu temple complex at Siddhpur town of Gujarat, India.
overview.description: The Rudra Mahalaya Temple, also known as Rudramal, is a destroyed/desecrated Hindu temple complex at Siddhpur town of Gujarat, India. Its construction was started in 943 CE by Mularaja and completed in 1140 CE by Jayasimha Siddharaja, a ruler of the Chaulukya dynasty. The Hindu temple was destroyed by the Sultan of Delhi, Alauddin Khalji, and later the Sultan of Gujarat, Ahmed Shah I (1410–1444) desecrated and substantially demolished the temple, and also converted part of it into the congregational mosque (Jami Masjid) of the city. topPlaces.name: Siddhpur
topPlaces.description: Siddhpur, also spelled Sidhpur, is a Town, municipality and headquarter of Sidhpur taluka in Patan district, in the Indian state of Gujarat. It is a historical place located on the bank of the endorheic Saraswati River, which is probably a remaining of the ancient Sarasvati River.

topPlaces.name: Shristhal Sangrahalay
topPlaces.description: Shristhal Sangrahalay, also known as Siddhpur Museum or Bindu Sarovar Museum, is a museum located near Bindu Sarovar in Siddhpur, Gujarat, India. Opened in 2017, it has three galleries presenting religious and ritual significance as well as history and society of the region.

topPlaces.name: Mudana
topPlaces.description: Mudana is a village in Patan district, Gujarat, India. In March 2009, it was affected by the 2009 Gujarat hepatitis outbreak.

topPlaces.name: Brahmanwada
topPlaces.description: Brahmanwada or Varwada is a village in Gujarat, India. It is located in the Unjha taluka of Mahesana district.

topPlaces.name: Nanosana
topPlaces.description: Nanosana is a small village in India, situated in Banaskantha district in northern Gujarat, India. Administratively, it is in Vadgam Taluka near post Nandotra.

items.note: Siddhpur, also spelled Sidhpur, is a Town, municipality and headquarter of Sidhpur taluka in Patan district, in the Indian state of Gujarat. It is a historical place located on the bank of the endorheic Saraswati River, which is probably a remaining of the ancient Sarasvati River.

Shristhal Sangrahalay, also known as Siddhpur Museum or Bindu Sarovar Museum, is a museum located near Bindu Sarovar in Siddhpur, Gujarat, India. Opened in 2017, it has three galleries presenting religious and ritual significance as well as history and society of the region.
Mudana is a village in Patan district, Gujarat, India. In March 2009, it was affected by the 2009 Gujarat hepatitis outbreak.
Brahmanwada or Varwada is a village in Gujarat, India. It is located in the Unjha taluka of Mahesana district.
Nanosana is a small village in India, situated in Banaskantha district in northern Gujarat, India. Administratively, it is in Vadgam Taluka near post Nandotra.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Rudra Mahalaya Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Rudra Mahalaya Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Rudra Mahalaya Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel International Airport, Ahmedabad (97 km) and the nearest railway station is Ahmedabad Junction (102 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Rudra Mahalaya Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Rudra Mahalaya Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Rudra Mahalaya Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Rudra Mahalaya Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Rudra Mahalaya Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Siddhpur, Shristhal Sangrahalay, Mudana, Brahmanwada, Nanosana.
